pages.text:
INSTRUMENT OF ACCESSION

WHEREAS, the Convention relating to International Exhibitions was done at

Paris on 22 November 1928 and was amended and supplemented by the

Protocol of 30 November 1972, the Amendments of 24 June 1982 and 31 May 1988.

AND WHEREAS Article 3 of the said Protocol provides that it shall remain open for signature by the Governments Parties to the 1928 Convention at Paris from

30. November 1972 until November 29, 1973 and thereafter shall remain open for accession by them.

AND WHEREAS, the terms of the said Convention relating to International

Exhibitions, the said Protocol and the said Amendments have been examined and found to be acceptable to the Government of the Democratic Socialist

Republic of Sri Lanka.

NOW, THEREFORE I, ROHITHA BOGOLLAGAMA, Minister of Foreign Affairs of the Democratic Socialist Republic of Sri Lanka declare its accession to the said Convention relating to International Exhibitions of Paris and the said

Protocol and undertake faithfully to perform and observe all its provisions and to take all such lawful measures as may be necessary to prevent the violation thereof.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF. I have signed these presents and affixed hereto my seal.

Done at Colombo this Tuesday the Fourth day of the Waning Moon of the month of Vap of the year Two Thousand Five Hundred and Fifty-One of the Buddhist

Era (Tuesday the 30"* day of the month of October of the year 2007 of the

Christian Era).

Mg..

RONÍTHA BOGOLLAGAMA, M.P.

Minister of Foreign Affairs
